question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/subscriber with a very long name path variable (e.g., 10,000 characters), causing excessive string comparison operations in the getName().equals(name) filter across all subscriptions and degrading API performance?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SubscribeMetrics ConcurrentHashMap durationMetrics growing unboundedly as new Scenario objects are created, by triggering repeated subscription creation cycles that add new entries without cleanup, causing a memory leak?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a PublishResponse where getRequest().getTimestamp() returns a far-future Instant, causing SubscribeMetrics.onNext() to compute a large negative latency Duration.between(publishedTimestamp, receivedTimestamp) that corrupts the Timer histogram with invalid samples?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RestSubscription sink's asFlux() in clientSubscribe() to cause backpressure overflow when the REST API is slow, resulting in dropped PublishResponse items that are silently ignored via onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty())?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/subscriber repeatedly at high frequency to cause the Flux.fromIterable(subscribers).flatMap(MirrorSubscriber::getSubscriptions) chain to be re-evaluated on each request, creating excessive reactive stream allocations and degrading JVM garbage collection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscription constructor called in createSubscriptions() with an attacker-controlled properties object to set an arbitrarily large subscribers count (up to 1024), creating 1024 parallel gRPC streams that overwhelm the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RestSubscriber.onPublish() method's subscriptions.filter().map().subscribe() chain to cause a reactive subscription leak if the inner subscribe() call is never disposed, accumulating dangling subscriptions over time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the take(properties.getLimit(), true) and take(properties.getDuration()) operators in RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe() by manipulating configuration to set limit=0 or duration=0, causing the Flux to immediately complete and preventing any REST monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/subscriber/{name}/{id} and exploit the Flux.last() operator which throws NoSuchElementException when the Flux is empty, causing the @ExceptionHandler to return a generic 404 that leaks the existence or non-existence of specific subscription names?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of mutual TLS or authentication on the gRPC connection in GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe() to intercept or replay subscription responses from the mirror node, injecting false SubscribeResponse data into the monitoring pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeSubscriber.onPublish() calls subscribers.forEach() without exception handling, so a RuntimeException thrown by one subscriber's onPublish() will prevent all subsequent subscribers from receiving the PublishResponse, causing partial monitoring blackout?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Scenario.onError(Throwable t) callback in RestSubscription to inject a Throwable with an extremely long message, causing the error to be stored in the getErrors() map and returned via the /api/v1/subscriber endpoint, leaking internal error details?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/subscriber with a status parameter list containing duplicate values, causing the status.get().contains(s.getStatus()) check to behave unexpectedly and potentially return subscriptions that should be filtered out?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SubscribeMetrics.status() scheduled method that logs scenario names and error maps, by causing a scenario with a crafted name to produce log entries that are misinterpreted by log parsers, enabling log injection attacks?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RestSubscriber.shouldSample() method's publishers set check by crafting a PublishResponse whose scenario name exactly matches a configured publisher name, bypassing the publisher filter and forcing sampling of attacker-controlled transactions?
